design_name: Rounded rectangular electronics enclosure tray, open-top, with 2mm shell walls, four corner mounting holes through the base, centered at origin on XY plane.
prompt: A rounded rectangular electronics enclosure body, open on top. Outer dimensions 80mm (X) by 50mm (Y) by 25mm tall (Z). The four vertical edges are rounded with an 8mm fillet radius, giving the enclosure a smooth rounded-rectangle cross-section. The top face (+Z) is removed and the body is shelled inward with 2mm wall thickness, creating a hollow tray with the outer dimensions preserved. Four 3.2mm diameter mounting holes are drilled through the bottom face, positioned in a rectangular pattern 64mm (X) by 34mm (Y) centered at the origin (each hole is 8mm inset from the nearest outer edges). The enclosure sits on the XY plane with its base at Z=0, centered at the origin.
primitive_plan_summary: Rounded rectangular electronics enclosure tray, open-top, with 2mm shell walls, four corner mounting holes through the base, centered at origin on XY plane.

verifier_score.feedback: All constraints met. The bounding box matches the specified 80×50×25mm outer dimensions exactly. The enclosure is centered at the origin with base at Z=0. The four vertical edges are filleted with 8mm radius, visible in all three rendered views as smooth rounded corners. The top face is open and the body is shelled inward with 2mm wall thickness, clearly visible in the center (top-down) view and the right (profile) view. Four 3.2mm mounting holes are visible in the bottom face in the center view, positioned in the correct 64×34mm rectangular pattern (±32, ±17). The volume of ~18,865 mm³ is physically plausible for a shelled enclosure of these dimensions. The geometry is valid, watertight, and has no self-intersections.
